name: Hugging Face Chat UI
tagline: Self-hosted chat interface for open LLMs, the SvelteKit app behind HuggingChat
seoTitle: Hugging Face Chat UI - Self-Hosted LLM Chat App
metaDescription: Hugging Face Chat UI is the open-source SvelteKit app behind HuggingChat. Self-host a chat interface over any OpenAI-compatible API and store history in MongoDB.
overview: Chat UI is the open-source SvelteKit application that powers HuggingChat at hf.co/chat. It gives you a web chat interface in front of large language models, and you run it on your own machine or server.

It talks to any OpenAI-compatible API through the OPENAI_BASE_URL and /models endpoints. That means it works with the Hugging Face Inference Providers router, a local llama.cpp server, Ollama, OpenRouter, and similar services without provider-specific code. Chat history, users, settings, and files are stored in MongoDB.

It fits the Chat UIs category as a full front end you host yourself, rather than a hosted product. It suits developers and teams who want a private chat app over open models, with control over branding, the model list, and where data is stored.
features: Works with any OpenAI-compatible API: Hugging Face router, llama.cpp, Ollama, OpenRouter, Poe, and others
Discovers available models from the ${OPENAI_BASE_URL}/models endpoint, with optional metadata overrides via the MODELS env var
Stores chat history, users, settings, files, and stats in MongoDB, with an embedded fallback that persists to ./db for local dev
Theming through environment variables (app name, assets, description, data-sharing toggle)
Optional server-side LLM routing via an 'Omni' alias that sends image inputs, MCP-tool requests, and other messages to configured routes
Ships an optional chat-ui-db Docker image that bundles MongoDB inside the container

gettingStarted.intro: Chat UI speaks to OpenAI-compatible APIs only. The fastest start uses the Hugging Face Inference Providers router with your personal access token.

steps.heading: Create .env.local
steps.body: Point Chat UI at an OpenAI-compatible endpoint and supply a matching key. This example uses the Hugging Face router.

steps.heading: Install and launch the dev server
steps.body: Clone the repo, install dependencies, and start the dev server. It opens in your browser so you can start chatting.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/huggingface/chat-ui
cd chat-ui
npm install
npm run dev -- --open

steps.heading: Database (optional for local dev)
steps.body: When MONGODB_URL is not set, Chat UI falls back to an embedded MongoDB that persists to ./db. To run MongoDB in a container, start one and point Chat UI at it.

steps.heading: Build for production
steps.body: Use the build and preview scripts to run a production build instead of the dev server.
steps.code:
npm run build
npm run preview

useCases: Self-host a private chat app over open models from the Hugging Face Hub for a team or organization
Put a browser chat interface in front of a local llama.cpp server or Ollama instance during development
Run a branded internal assistant where chat history and user data stay in your own MongoDB
Deploy a containerized chat app quickly using the chat-ui-db Docker image that bundles MongoDB
